observations.recommendation:
1.13 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
create an inventory committee that will facilitate the conduct of the
physical count of its PPE as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated 31 January 2020. In addition, to instruct the following in
relation thereto, to wit:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE, in compliance
with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
b. General Services Officer to maintain and update her PCs per
category of PPE, pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I;
c. Municipal Accountant to maintain and update her PPELCs per
category of PPE, pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I; and
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant to reconcile their PCs and PPELCs, respectively.

2.5 Having considered the above-cited situation, we recommended to the
Municipal Mayor to instruct the General Services Office to prepare
and submit the SSMI for Calendar Year 2022, pursuant to Section 121,
Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipality procure its CSEs from the PS-
DBM. If the BAC of the Municipality desires to procure its CSE other
than the said depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic
viability and/or non-availability of stocks in such depot, the procuring
entity must secure first a CNAS from such depot.

4.6 We recommended that the concerned JO workers be required to submit their
individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate
supervisors, in adherence to Section 4 (6) of Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445,
otherwise known as the “Government Auditing Code of the Philippines” and
Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.. This is to ensure
that the paid wages of the JO workers were rendered in accordance with their
assigned tasks and their services were truly necessary.

Hence, we highly recommended that the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee which shall compose of adequate number of members who shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
After which, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the:
1. Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and accordingly conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) following the guidelines set forth in said COA Circular;
2. General Services Officer to update the GSO’s property records based on the RPCPPE; and
3. General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective property records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the PPE.

4. As a result, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor to instruct the General Services Office to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) for Calendar Year 2021, in compliance with Section 121 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.

5. Likewise, we recommended that the Management shall provide controls to monitor the status of appropriations for the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses in order to avoid a potential overdraft on its allotment or disbursement, pursuant to Section 325 (h) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991.”

6. Therefore, we recommended that the Municipality should procure its common-use supplies and equipment (CSEs) from the Procurement Service - Department of Budget and Management (PS-DBM). If the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) of the Municipality desires to procure its CSEs other than the said depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of stocks in such depot, the procuring entity must secure first a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS) from such depot.

7. In relation to the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipality shall submit the copy of all contracts entered by the Management within five (5) working days upon perfection to the Office of the Auditor for review and evaluation, pursuant to Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Hence, we highly recommend that the Municipal Mayor to create an
Inventory Committee which shall compose of adequate number of
members who shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties
to devote their full time to be able to complete the physical inventory in
three months or less, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020. After which, it is recommended that the
Municipal Mayor to direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and
accordingly conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all
PPE of the Municipality and prepare the corresponding Report
on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) following the guidelines set forth in said COA
Circular;
b. General Services Officer to update the GSO’s property records
based on the RPCPPE; and
c. General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile
their respective property records to come up with the reconciled
balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance
of the PPE.

As a result, we recommend to the Municipal Mayor to instruct the
General Services Office to prepare and submit the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) for Calendar Year 2021, in
compliance with Section 121 of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.

Likewise, we recommend that the Management shall provide controls
to monitor the status of appropriations for the Extraordinary and
Miscellaneous Expenses in order to avoid a potential overdraft on its
allotment or disbursement, pursuant to Section 325 (h) of the Republic
Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code
of 1991.”

Therefore, we recommend that the Municipality should procure its
common-use supplies and equipment (CSEs) from the Procurement
Service - Department of Budget and Management (PS-DBM). If the
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) of the Municipality desires to
procure its CSEs other than the said depot on the grounds of efficiency,
practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of stocks in such
depot, the procuring entity must secure first a Certificate of Non-
Availability of Stocks (CNAS) from such depot.

In relation the foregoing, we recommend that the Bids and Award
Committee (BAC) prepares the Procurement Monitoring Report
(PMR) with the approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity and
submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within
fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester and the
Annual Procurement Plan to GPPB on or before end of January of the
budget year.

In relation to the foregoing, we recommend that the Municipality shall
submit the copy of all contracts entered by the Management within five
(5) working days upon perfection to the Office of the Auditor for review
and evaluation, pursuant to Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of the Commission
on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

a. To conduct physical inventory of PPE for CY 2020, pursuant to the Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I; b. Prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Audit Team in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and also to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with that of the accounting records; and c. Make the necessary adjustments thereon.
observations.managementAction: The General Service Officer (GSO), Phoebe B. Pimentel, is concurrently acting municipal treasurer since April 2018 to April 2020 and currently the OIC Municipal Accountant from May 2020. Thus, our HRMO, Salahudin Badawi, act as GSO. We couldn’t deny the fact that GSO personnel haven’t enough knowledge on how to conduct proper physical inventory.
observations.reason: The General Service Officer (GSO), Phoebe B. Pimentel, is concurrently acting municipal treasurer since April 2018 to April 2020 and currently the OIC Municipal Accountant from May 2020. Thus, our HRMO, Salahudin Badawi, act as GSO. We couldn’t deny the fact that GSO personnel haven’t enough knowledge on how to conduct proper physical inventory.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive (LCE) should instruct the TED focal person to monitor the implementati on of the Gender and Development (GAD) to comply with the foregoing provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 of PWC, DILG, DBM and NEDA and other relevant laws, rules and regulations related to GAD Planning and Budgeting.

The Local Chief Executive (LCE) should instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of affected accounts by following the relevant procedures cited-above.
Procure directly to PS-DBM for the acquisition of the common-use supplies, if the same were not available, then secure a certification for the non-availability of stocks from the said office.
observations.managementAction: The Management will procure its CSE from the nearby PS-DBM depot starting Calendar Year 2022.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation

a. To conduct of physical inventory of its PPE for CY 2020, pursuant to Section
156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October
20, 1992;
b. Prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Audit Team in accordance
with Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and also to the Municipal
Accountant for reconciliation with that of the accounting records; and
c. Make the necessary adjustments thereon.

Recommendation

The Local Chief Executive (LCE) should instruct the focal person to
monitor the implementation of the Gender and Development (GAD) to
comply with the foregoing provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC)
No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 of PWC, DILG, DBM and NEDA and
other relevant laws, rules and regulations related to GAD Planning and
Budgeting.

Recommendation

The Local Chief Executive (LCE) should instruct the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the
balances of affected accounts by following the relevant procedures cited-
above.

Recommendation

Procure directly to PS-DBM for the acquisition of common-use
supplies, if the same were not available, then secure a certification for the
non-availability of stocks from the said office.

We recommend the following: a. Conduct of physical inventory of its PPE for CY 2019, pursuant to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; b. Prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and c. Make the necessary adjustments thereon.
observations.managementAction: The General Service Officer (GSO), Phoebe B. Pimentel, is concurrently the municipal treasurer since April 2018 to April 2020 and currently the OIC Municipal Accountant from May 2020. Thus, Municipality. Our HRMO, Salahudin Badawi, act as GSO. We couldn’t deny the fact that GSO personnel haven’t enough knowledge on how to conduct physical inventory. Therefore, our office are asking for your help and assistant on how to conduct appropriate physical inventory of PPE so that we could submit proper report and avoid problem in the future.
observations.reason: The General Service Officer is concurrently the municipal treasurer since April 2018 to April 2020 and the acting Inventory Committee has not been created/convened; GSO personnel haven’t enough knowledge on how to conduct physical inventory and assistance is being requested.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct its personnel concerned to liquidate their respective cash advances, pursuant to Section 5.1 and 5.8 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The cash advance amounted to P 600,000.00 that is not liquidated at the end of the Fiscal Year 2019. The cash advance is the only cash advance that is not liquidated at the end of the Fiscal Year 2019. Accordingly, she will liquidate the said amount as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: a. Abide to the Memorandum of Understanding between the Department of Agrarian Reform (DAR-Italian) and Municipality of Datu Saudi Ampatuan, Maguindanao which stipulated that the maintenance cost should be shouldered by the concerned local government unit; b. Allocate funds for the proper maintenance of the water system; c. Maintain the water system for its prolong utilization for the benefit of the intended users; and d. Rehabilitate portion of the water system, which was damaged through long months of drought and destroyed during the sub-grading of the streets along the perimeter fence of the said municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Local Government Unit hired One (1) Local Water Technician to task the maintenance of the system; a. The Local Water Technician will conduct daily inspection of the system from the source to the tap-stand; b. However during the month’s long drought the source 2 has been shortage; b. The water distributions at the Housing Project back of the Pink Masjid was insufficient.
observations.reason: During the visit conducted, the information gathered was not properly validated and source from the technical management of this LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following:

a. Conduct of physical inventory of its PPE for CY 2019, pursuant to Section 156
of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992;
b. Prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for
reconciliation with that of the accounting records; and
c. Make the necessary adjustments thereon.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct its personnel
concerned to liquidate their respective cash advances, pursuant to Section 5.1 and
5.8 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10,
1997.

We recommend the management the following:

a. Abide to the Memorandum of Understanding between the Department of
Agrarian Reform (DAR-Italian) and Municipality of Datu Saudi Ampatuan,
Maguindanao which stipulated that the maintenance cost should be shouldered
by the concerned local government unit;
b. Allocate funds for the proper maintenance of the water system;
c. Maintain the water system for its prolong utilization for the benefit of the
intended users; and
d. Rehabilitate portion of the water system, which was damaged through long
months of drought and destroyed during the sub-grading of the streets along the
perimeter fence of the said municipality.

The Local Executive must see to it that the Accountant and the Treasurer of the agency will regularly submit to the Audit Team the Bank Reconciliation Report together with the bank statements of the General and Trust Funds.

The management is advised to submit physical accomplishment reports for the two Farm to Market Roads. The report accomplishments must contain evidence (picture) clearly showing the actual status of the projects.

The Local Executive must see to it that the Accountant and the Treasurer of the agency will regularly submit to the Audit Team the Bank Reconciliation Report together with the bank statements of the General and Trust Funds.

Recommendation:
The management is advised to submit physical accomplishment reports for the two Farm to Market Roads projects. The report must contain evidence (pictures) clearly showing the actual status of the projects. And a request for inspection of the projects.

Recommendation:
The management should submit report on the status of the projects with pictures and a request for inspection.

Recommendation:
The agency is advised to submit required documents and reports adhering to RA 9184.

Require the Municipal General Service Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records, considering some accounts that cannot be located and its date of acquisition cannot be determined.
observations.reason: Lack of working personnel and time constraint

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately all cash collections in his custody and, henceforth, the same should be deposited intact on a daily basis, or at least once a week together with the required Report of Collection and Deposit.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer has other assignment or attending to meetings and seminars.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended for the LGU to fully adhere to the DILG-DBM joint circular of April 13, 2011.

The Chief Executive shall direct the preparations and submission of all documentary requirements for the procurement of Infrastructure Projects on the following officials: The Municipal Engineer and Accountant.
Continue the Good Performance.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the Disbursement vouchers and RCI of the Trust and Special Educational Funds for the CY 2015 together with the JEVs

Require the Municipal General Services Officer to conduct physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE and to coordinate with Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.
Open a Special Trust Fund accont purposely for the unexpended balance at year-end. Transfer to this accont the unexpended balance of LDRRM Funds as of December 31, 2015.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately all cash collections in his custody and, henceforth, the same should  be deposited intact on a daily basis, or at least once a week to avoid loss or misuse.

Observe strictly the due dates for the remittance of BIR, GSIS, PHIC, HDMF in order to update the records of the employees and to avoid any surcharges or penalties that might b e imposed due to the delay.

Instruct the Municipal accountant and Treasurer to submit the remaining DVs for the last quarter of 2015. Henceforth, strictly observe the due dates for the submission of the DVs and avoid the long delays in the submission.
Verify the status of these checks. If these were not cancelled, submit the DVs together with the corresponding checks. If cancelled, submit to the Audit Team the duplicate copies of the checks.
